steps.title: Find the key contrast in the passage
steps.content: The passage sets up a rule: traveling waves break when steepness passes a critical threshold, so "breaking thus constrains wave height." Then it delivers the exception: McAllister et al. found that "spike waves can exceed that constraint" because other factors (jet stability, cavity shape) govern their breaking. The main idea is this exception — spike waves can be taller than traveling-wave physics would predict.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices and confirm D
steps.content:
• Choice A — Previous studies have suggested that steepness mediates breaking in traveling waves, but the study by McAllister et al. shows that jet stability and cavity shape may also influence breaking in such waves.: Mis-assigns jet stability and cavity shape to traveling waves; the text says those factors govern spike-wave breaking, not traveling-wave breaking. ✗
  • Choice B — The process of breaking limits the height of traveling waves, but the study by McAllister et al. suggests that spike waves can exceed those limits if their height-to-length ratio reaches a critical threshold.: Wrongly states spike waves exceed limits by reaching a steepness threshold; the passage says they exceed the constraint because additional factors mediate their breaking. ✗
  • Choice C — McAllister et al. suggest that spike waves can form when traveling waves propagating in opposing directions intersect and that spike waves tend to be higher than traveling waves.: The formation mechanism is background fact, not the researchers' finding; the text also never claims spike waves are generally higher. ✗
  • Choice D — The study by McAllister et al. suggests that when traveling waves intersect in specific ways, the resulting wave may be higher than would be expected based on the properties of traveling waves.: Correctly captures the main idea: spike waves can exceed the height constraint set by traveling-wave steepness rules. ✓
That matches Choice D — The study by McAllister et al. suggests that when traveling waves intersect in specific ways, the resulting wave may be higher than would be expected based on the properties of traveling waves..
